package com.example.service;

import javax.crypto.Cipher;
import javax.crypto.spec.IvParameterSpec;

import java.security.MessageDigest;

import java.util.Arrays;

/**

public class AESService {

    /**
     * Encrypts a plaintext message using AES encryption
     *
     * @param key  The encryption key string
     * @param data The byte array of the input message
     * @return Returns the ciphertext as a hexadecimal string after encryption
     * @throws Exception Exceptions that may occur during encryption
     */
    public static String encrypt(String key, byte[] data) throws Exception {
        if (key == null || key.isEmpty()) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Encryption key cannot be empty.");
        }
        if (data == null || data.length == 0) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Data to be encrypted cannot be empty.");
        }

        try {
            // Hash the key using SHA-256 and truncate to 256 bits (32 bytes) for use as an AES key
            MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256");
            byte[] keyBytes = digest.digest(key.getBytes("UTF-8"));
            keyBytes = Arrays.copyOf(keyBytes, 32); // Ensure 256-bit key

            // Generate a random IV for CBC mode
            SecureRandom random = new SecureRandom();
            byte[] ivBytes = new byte[16];
            random.nextBytes(ivBytes);
            IvParameterSpec ivParameterSpec = new IvParameterSpec(ivBytes);

            // Initialize cipher in CBC mode with PKCS5 padding
            Cipher cipher = Cipher.getInstance("AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding");
            cipher.init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, secretKeySpec, ivParameterSpec);

            byte[] encryptedBytes = cipher.doFinal(data);

            // Prepend IV to ciphertext
            byte[] ivAndCiphertext = new byte[ivBytes.length + encryptedBytes.length];
            System.arraycopy(ivBytes, 0, ivAndCiphertext, 0, ivBytes.length);
            System.arraycopy(encryptedBytes, 0, ivAndCiphertext, ivBytes.length, encryptedBytes.length);

            // Convert to hexadecimal string

            for (byte b : ivAndCiphertext) {
                sb.append(String.format("%02x", b));
            }
            return sb.toString();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            throw new Exception("AES encryption failed: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        }
    }
}

import java.security.InvalidAlgorithmParameterException;
import java.security.InvalidKeyException;
import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;
import java.security.SecureRandom;
import java.util.Base64;
import javax.crypto.BadPaddingException;
import javax.crypto.Cipher;
import javax.crypto.IllegalBlockSizeException;
import javax.crypto.spec.GCMParameterSpec;
import javax.crypto.spec.SecretKeySpec;

/**
 * AES encryption utility class for secure message transmission.
 * Implements AES/GCM/NoPadding mode with automatic IV generation.
 * Uses Base64-encoded key input and outputs hex-encoded ciphertext.
 */
public class AESService {

    private static final String ALGORITHM = "AES";
    private static final String TRANSFORMATION = "AES/GCM/NoPadding";
    private static final int GCM_IV_LENGTH = 12; // 96 bits for GCM
    private static final int GCM_TAG_LENGTH = 128; // 128-bit authentication tag
    private static final String HEX_CHARS = "0123456789abcdef";

    /**
     * Encrypts data using AES with GCM mode and returns hex-encoded ciphertext.
     * 
     * @param key  Base64-encoded AES key (must be 16, 24, or 32 bytes)
     * @param data Plaintext bytes to encrypt
     * @return Hex-encoded ciphertext (IV + encrypted data + authentication tag)
     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if key length is invalid
     * @throws RuntimeException if encryption fails due to cryptographic errors
     */
    public static String encrypt(String key, byte[] data) {
        try {
            // Validate input parameters
            if (key == null || key.isEmpty()) {
                throw new IllegalArgumentException("Key cannot be null or empty");
            }
            if (data == null) {
                throw new IllegalArgumentException("Data cannot be null");
            }

            // Decode Base64-encoded key
            byte[] keyBytes;
            try {
                keyBytes = Base64.getDecoder().decode(key);
            } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
                throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid Base64-encoded key", e);
            }
            
            // Validate key length for AES
            if (keyBytes.length != 16 && keyBytes.length != 24 && keyBytes.length != 32) {
                throw new IllegalArgumentException(
                    "Invalid key length: " + keyBytes.length + " bytes. Must be 16, 24, or 32 bytes.");
            }

            SecretKeySpec secretKey = new SecretKeySpec(keyBytes, ALGORITHM);

            // Generate cryptographically secure random IV
            byte[] iv = new byte[GCM_IV_LENGTH];
            SecureRandom random = new SecureRandom();
            random.nextBytes(iv);

            // Initialize cipher for AES/GCM/NoPadding
            Cipher cipher = Cipher.getInstance(TRANSFORMATION);
            GCMParameterSpec gcmSpec = new GCMParameterSpec(GCM_TAG_LENGTH, iv);
            cipher.init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, secretKey, gcmSpec);

            // Perform encryption
            byte[] encryptedData = cipher.doFinal(data);

            // Combine IV and encrypted data for output
            byte[] combined = new byte[iv.length + encryptedData.length];
            System.arraycopy(iv, 0, combined, 0, iv.length);
            System.arraycopy(encryptedData, 0, combined, iv.length, encryptedData.length);

            return bytesToHex(combined);
            
        } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException | NoSuchPaddingException e) {
            throw new RuntimeException("AES algorithm not available: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        } catch (InvalidKeyException e) {
            throw new RuntimeException("Invalid encryption key: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        } catch (InvalidAlgorithmParameterException e) {
            throw new RuntimeException("Invalid GCM parameters: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        } catch (IllegalBlockSizeException | BadPaddingException e) {
            throw new RuntimeException("Encryption padding error: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        }
    }

    /**
     * Converts byte array to lowercase hexadecimal string representation.
     * 
     * @param bytes Input byte array
     * @return Hexadecimal string representation
     */
    private static String bytesToHex(byte[] bytes) {
        if (bytes == null || bytes.length == 0) {
            return "";
        }
        
        char[] hexChars = new char[bytes.length * 2];
        for (int i = 0; i < bytes.length; i++) {
            int v = bytes[i] & 0xFF;
            hexChars[i * 2] = HEX_CHARS.charAt(v >>> 4);
            hexChars[i * 2 + 1] = HEX_CHARS.charAt(v & 0x0F);
        }
        return new String(hexChars);
    }
}

package com.example.service;

import javax.crypto.Cipher;
import javax.crypto.spec.GCMParameterSpec;
import javax.crypto.spec.SecretKeySpec;
import java.security.SecureRandom;
import java.util.Base64;

/**
 * AES encryption utility service for secure message transmission
 * Uses AES-GCM for authenticated encryption
 */
public class AESService {

    private static final int GCM_IV_LENGTH = 12; // 96 bits for GCM
    private static final int GCM_TAG_LENGTH = 128; // 128-bit authentication tag

    /**
     * Encrypts the provided data using AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption.
     * 
     * @param key  Base64-encoded AES key (128, 192, or 256-bit)
     * @param data Plaintext data to encrypt
     * @return Hexadecimal string of encrypted data (IV + ciphertext + tag)
     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if key is invalid or data is null
     * @throws RuntimeException for any encryption errors
     */
    public static String encrypt(String key, byte[] data) {
        try {
            // Validate input parameters
            if (key == null || key.trim().isEmpty()) {
                throw new IllegalArgumentException("Key cannot be null or empty");
            }
            if (data == null) {
                throw new IllegalArgumentException("Data cannot be null");
            }

            // Decode base64 key and validate length
            byte[] keyBytes = Base64.getDecoder().decode(key);
            int keyLength = keyBytes.length * 8; // Convert bytes to bits
            
            // Validate key length (AES supports 128, 192, 256 bits)
            if (keyLength != 128 && keyLength != 192 && keyLength != 256) {
                throw new IllegalArgumentException("Key must be 128, 192, or 256 bits (16, 24, or 32 bytes after base64 decoding)");
            }

            // Generate random IV for GCM
            byte[] iv = new byte[GCM_IV_LENGTH];
            SecureRandom secureRandom = new SecureRandom();
            secureRandom.nextBytes(iv);

            // Initialize cipher for AES-GCM encryption
            SecretKeySpec secretKey = new SecretKeySpec(keyBytes, "AES");
            Cipher cipher = Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding");
            GCMParameterSpec gcmSpec = new GCMParameterSpec(GCM_TAG_LENGTH, iv);
            cipher.init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, secretKey, gcmSpec);

            // Encrypt the data
            byte[] encryptedData = cipher.doFinal(data);

            // Combine IV, encrypted data, and authentication tag
            byte[] combined = new byte[iv.length + encryptedData.length];
            System.arraycopy(iv, 0, combined, 0, iv.length);
            System.arraycopy(encryptedData, 0, combined, iv.length, encryptedData.length);

            // Convert to hexadecimal string efficiently
            return bytesToHex(combined);

        } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
            throw e; // Re-throw validation exceptions
        } catch (Exception e) {
            throw new RuntimeException("Encryption failed: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        }
    }

    /**
     * Converts byte array to hexadecimal string efficiently
     */
    private static String bytesToHex(byte[] bytes) {
        char[] hexChars = new char[bytes.length * 2];
        final char[] hexArray = "0123456789ABCDEF".toCharArray();
        
        for (int i = 0; i < bytes.length; i++) {
            int v = bytes[i] & 0xFF;
            hexChars[i * 2] = hexArray[v >>> 4];
            hexChars[i * 2 + 1] = hexArray[v & 0x0F];
        }
        
        return new String(hexChars);
    }
